summary: Added a GitHub Actions `build-ninja` job to `.github/workflows/build-melee.yml` so the doldecomp/melee repository CI builds with `ninja` inside the existing `ghcr.io/doldecomp/build-melee:main` container. The job checks out a sparse subset of the repo, configures GALE01 with compilers from `/compilers`, runs both normal and `all_source` ninja builds, uploads progress on the default branch, and stores generated MAP files as artifacts.
what_changed: Added a new `build-ninja` GitHub Actions job named `Ninja`.
Restricted the job to run only for `github.repository == 'doldecomp/melee'`.
Introduced a build matrix over `version: [GALE01]` and `options: ["", "all_source"]` with `fail-fast: false`.
Uses `actions/checkout@v3` with `fetch-depth: 1` and sparse checkout limited to `src`, `tools`, and `config`.
Adds a prepare step creating `ln -s /orig .`, relying on the container-provided original assets path.
Configures the build via `python configure.py` using `--version ${{matrix.version}} --compilers /compilers`, and adds `--map` only for the default empty-options build.
Runs `ninja` with matrix options, including an `all_source` build variant.
Uploads progress JSON to `https://progress.decomp.club/` only for the default options build on the repository default branch, using `PROGRESS_API_KEY`.
Uploads generated `*.MAP` files as an artifact named `${{matrix.version}}_maps` for the default options build.
smart_moves: Used a matrix to exercise both the standard build and the `all_source` build without duplicating job definitions.
Kept CI checkout lightweight with sparse checkout of only `src`, `tools`, and `config`.
Avoided failing the whole CI run due to progress upload problems by marking the upload step `continue-on-error: true`.
Limited progress uploads to the default branch and the non-`all_source` matrix entry, preventing noisy or duplicate uploads from PR branches and alternate build modes.
Only generated and uploaded maps for the standard build by conditionally adding `--map` when matrix options are empty.
Pinned the build environment to the project container image so the ninja build has consistent compilers and original assets paths.
decomp_lessons: For decomp CI, test both normal matching output and `all_source` output because they validate different build paths.
Progress reporting should be separated from build correctness: upload failures should not mask whether the decomp build itself passed.
Map artifact upload is useful for later inspection, but should be scoped to the build variant that actually requests map generation.
Sparse checkout can substantially reduce CI overhead when build configuration only needs known directories.
Containerized CI can standardize access to compilers and `/orig`, reducing local-environment drift.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: No direct assembly or C matching tactics were present; this PR was CI/build tooling only.
The build step uses `configure.py --map` for the standard build, which supports generation of MAP files useful for matching/progress inspection.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs modifying `.github/workflows/build-melee.yml` or the `build-ninja` job.
Search for CI failures or fixes related to `ninja all_source`.
Check whether `actions/checkout@v3` or `actions/upload-artifact@v3` were later upgraded.
Look for changes to `tools/upload_progress.py` or progress JSON generation assumptions.
Search for additional versions beyond `GALE01` added to the ninja CI matrix.
